formal_definition: A virtual asset that falls within the definition of "securities" under the Securities and Futures Ordinance (Cap. 571), triggering SFO-licensing requirements (in addition to any AMLO requirement) for a platform trading it.
plain_definition: A virtual asset that legally counts as a "security" -- for example, one representing shares, a debt, or a right to profits -- and so falls under Hong Kong's existing securities law, unlike a "non-security token" (such as bitcoin), which does not represent any of those rights. A platform trading security tokens needs an SFC licence for dealing in securities; a platform trading non-security tokens instead needs an AMLO licence as a virtual asset exchange. Because a token's classification can change over time, Hong Kong's regulator considers it prudent for platforms to hold both licences.
